steps.title: Substitute the triangle's side lengths into the equation
steps.content:
The equation 2x + y = 37 involves the sides of triangle QRS, where QR = x, RS = x, and SQ = y. We can rewrite the left side:

$$2x + y = x + x + y = \text{QR} + \text{RS} + \text{SQ}$$

This shows that 2x + y is the sum of all three side lengths of the triangle.

steps.title: Recognize that the sum of the three sides is the perimeter
steps.content: The sum of all three side lengths of a triangle is called the perimeter. Since 2x + y = 37, the value 37 represents the perimeter of triangle QRS—the sum of the lengths of the three sides. That matches choice C — C is correct.
